name: Sureshvara
dates_notes: Direct disciple of Śaṅkara; identification with pre-conversion Maṇḍana is disputed (Hiriyanna negative; traditional sectarian sources affirmative).

lineage_polemical.note: Sureśvara's jñāna-only / Brahman-as-locus-of-avidyā position is structurally opposed to Maṇḍana's bhāvanā / jīva-as-locus-of-avidyā stream.

core_thesis: Sureśvara, Śaṅkara's direct disciple, holds that *brahman*-knowledge — *akhaṇḍākāra-vṛtti* arising from *mahāvākya*-cognition (paradigmatically *tat tvam asi*) — is itself directly liberating, requiring no supplementary *prasaṅkhyāna* / repeated meditative *bhāvanā* (this is precisely the point on which he opposes Maṇḍana). *Avidyā* is one (not plural) and is located in *brahman* (not the *jīva*), since the *jīva* is itself a product of *avidyā* and cannot be its substrate without circularity. *Karma* and *jñāna* belong to incommensurable epistemic orders: *karma* presupposes the agent–object structure that *jñāna* dissolves, so any *jñāna-karma-samuccaya* (combination of knowledge and ritual action) as a path to *mokṣa* is incoherent — a position he develops at length against Maṇḍana's *Brahma-Siddhi* and against the Bhartṛprapañca stream. The pure *naiṣkarmya* (action-less) state of *jñāna* is *mokṣa* itself. This stream — *brahman*-locus *avidyā*, *vākya*-cognition as immediately sufficient, strict *jñāna*-only — is the proto-template that Padmapāda's Vivaraṇa lineage and Sarvajñātman will systematize into the *Vivaraṇa* sub-school.

engaged_works.title: Naishkarmya-Siddhi
engaged_works.summary: Independent *prakaraṇa* in four chapters, mixed verse and prose, defending the *naiṣkarmya* (action-less) reading of liberation against the *jñāna-karma-samuccaya* alternative. Chapter 1 establishes *brahman*-knowledge as the sole means of *mokṣa* and refutes the view that Vedic action even of the most refined kind contributes to liberation. Chapter 2 analyzes the *mahāvākya tat tvam asi*, the *jahad-ajahad-lakṣaṇā* (joint inclusive-exclusive implication) by which the *vākya* is read non-dually, and the *adhyāsa*-mechanism. Chapter 3 takes up specific *pūrvapakṣa* objections — Maṇḍana's *prasaṅkhyāna-vāda* is the principal target — and argues that *vākya*-cognition itself is *aparokṣa* (immediate) and self-sufficient. Chapter 4 treats *jīvanmukti* and the residual conditions of the liberated knower. Securely authored; the work is the systematic doctrinal complement to Sureśvara's *Vārttikas*. Critical edition: Hino / R. Balasubramanian.

engaged_works.title: Brhadaranyaka-Varttika
engaged_works.ascription_notes: Securely authored vārttika on Śaṅkara's Bṛhadāraṇyaka-Bhāṣya.
engaged_works.summary: Massive *vārttika* in verse — over eleven thousand stanzas — on Śaṅkara's Bṛhadāraṇyaka-Upaniṣad-Bhāṣya, organized by *adhyāya* and *brāhmaṇa* following the underlying Upaniṣadic and *bhāṣya* structure. The *Sambandha-Vārttika* (the introductory portion glossing the *bhāṣya* preface) is doctrinally pivotal: Sureśvara uses it to develop, at length, the relation of *karma* to *jñāna*, the impossibility of *jñāna-karma-samuccaya*, and the *brahman*-locus of *avidyā* — positions for which there is *bhāṣya* warrant but which receive their first systematic exposition here. The work is the principal vehicle for the *Vivaraṇa*-stream's elaboration of Śaṅkara's positions and is the most cited *vārttika* in later Advaita literature. It also preserves substantial fragments of Bhartṛprapañca via the *pūrvapakṣa* portions. Securely authored; the standard Ānandāśrama edition is the working text.

engaged_works.title: Taittiriya-Varttika
engaged_works.ascription_notes: Securely authored vārttika on Śaṅkara's Taittirīya-Bhāṣya.
engaged_works.summary: Verse *vārttika* on Śaṅkara's Taittirīya-Upaniṣad-Bhāṣya, organized by the three *vallīs* of the underlying Upaniṣad. The most doctrinally fertile portion treats *Brahmānanda-Vallī*: Sureśvara develops at length the analysis of *satyaṃ jñānam anantaṃ brahma* (Tait. 2.1.1) as *svarūpa-lakṣaṇa* (definition by essential nature) rather than predication of independent *guṇas*; defends Śaṅkara's reading of *ānanda-maya-kośa* as still belonging to the *upādhi*-order rather than as identical with *brahman*; and elaborates the relation of the *kośa*-hierarchy to the *avasthā-traya* and to *adhyāsa*. The work is shorter and tighter than the Bṛhadāraṇyaka-Vārttika and is treated by later Advaita as the standard *vārttika* on the *pañca-kośa* analysis. Securely authored; standard Ānandāśrama edition. The Taittirīya- and Bṛhadāraṇyaka-Vārttikas together constitute Sureśvara's *vārttika* corpus on Śaṅkara's Upaniṣad-bhāṣyas.
